mnemonics: ADABoost: ADAptively Boosts weak learners by focusing on mistakes. Think of it as a student (the model) who only studies the questions they got wrong on the last test (the errors), getting progressively better with each round of studying (each iteration).
keyPoints: Boosting is an ensemble method that combines multiple weak learners (models that do slightly better than random guessing) into a single strong learner.
It works sequentially, with each new model attempting to correct the errors made by the previous models in the ensemble.
Models are weighted based on their performance, and final predictions are made through a weighted majority vote (classification) or a weighted sum (regression).
commonMistakes: Confusing Boosting with Bagging (e.g., Random Forest). Bagging builds models in parallel on bootstrapped samples, while Boosting builds them sequentially on weighted data.
Believing that subsequent models are built on a completely new dataset. Instead, the data is re-weighted, putting more emphasis on previously misclassified instances.
